operating_ranges.evidence: Tube exchange required when deposition reaches approximately 20 microns

degradation_mechanisms.rate_description: depends on particle additions during processing
degradation_mechanisms.evidence: Tube exchange required when deposition reaches approximately 20 microns. Timing strongly depends on particle additions during processing
If particles exceed specifications, tube should be exchanged

fault_conditions.name: excessive_deposition
fault_conditions.description: deposition thickness reaches approximately 20 microns requiring tube exchange
fault_conditions.evidence: Tube exchange required when deposition reaches approximately 20 microns

fault_conditions.name: particle_contamination
fault_conditions.description: particles exceed specifications requiring tube exchange
fault_conditions.evidence: If particles exceed specifications, tube should be exchanged

maintenance_actions.name: tube_change
maintenance_actions.evidence: Tube exchange required when deposition reaches approximately 20 microns

maintenance_actions.name: pre_cleaning_hf_treatment
maintenance_actions.evidence: 1:10 HF/DI-water ratio for 2-3 minutes. Rinse in DI-water, dry with dry nitrogen

maintenance_actions.name: fiberfax_wool_repacking
maintenance_actions.evidence: after every tube change, if a gap exists between the process tube and the adapter, it must be repacked with fiberfax wool This will prevent air movement within the furnace and resultant temperature cycling

assumptions.statement: Process tube operates in controlled atmosphere during deposition
assumptions.reason: Component role states it provides controlled atmosphere and manual shows gas flow controls and process recipes

assumptions.statement: Tube degradation is primarily from material deposition rather than thermal cycling
assumptions.reason: Manual emphasizes deposition thickness as primary driver for tube exchange
